notes: Spec asserts: the agy session stays alive past readyTTL (PID-liveness via ExactName{agy}), state remains ready during idle, and the next user prompt resumes ready→working. Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15s duration_at_least_ms cleanly exceeds the TTL. If PID-liveness regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (no transcript_removed, no flicker to working). Birth is the presession proc-<PID> → conv-id session pair; the idle/turn phases pin to the conv-id session. Observations omit cost/tokens (no usage in antigravity's JSONL).

text: Session appears in ready (presession proc-<PID> or conv-id session, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL; state remains ready while agy is alive and idle
Next user prompt: ready → working resumes normally
Second turn completes back to ready
